Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. US economy sheds 92,000 jobs in February in sharp slide 100 upvotes, 12 comments
  2. Querying 3B Vectors 68 upvotes, 9 comments
  3. Galileo's handwritten notes found in ancient astronomy text 166 upvotes, 32 comments
  4. CT Scans of Health Wearables 231 upvotes, 48 comments
  5. 48x32, a 1536 LED Game Computer (2023) 34 upvotes, 4 comments
  6. QGIS 4.0 118 upvotes, 26 comments
  7. Plasma Bigscreen – 10-foot interface for KDE plasma 519 upvotes, 159 comments
  8. Ki Editor - an editor that operates on the AST 125 upvotes, 32 comments
  9. Editing changes in patch format with Jujutsu 40 upvotes, 7 comments
  10. this css proves me human 305 upvotes, 97 comments
  11. Sarvam 105B, the first competitive Indian open source LLM 111 upvotes, 31 comments
  12. Self-Portrait by Ernst Mach (1886) 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  13. Lock Scroll with a Vengeance 29 upvotes, 6 comments
  14. Helix: A post-modern text editor 213 upvotes, 90 comments
  15. The Case of the Disappearing Secretary 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  16. Working and Communicating with Japanese Engineers 69 upvotes, 32 comments
  17. Show HN: Moongate – Ultima Online server emulator in .NET 10 with Lua scripting 265 upvotes, 153 comments
  18. Tech employment now significantly worse than the 2008 or 2020 recessions 945 upvotes, 623 comments
  19. Filesystems Are Having a Moment 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  20. Boy I was wrong about the Fediverse 88 upvotes, 48 comments
  21. UUID package coming to Go standard library 251 upvotes, 161 comments
  22. Uploading Pirated Books via BitTorrent Qualifies as Fair Use, Meta Argues 144 upvotes, 91 comments
  23. LLMs work best when the user defines their acceptance criteria first 299 upvotes, 216 comments
  24. Compiling Match Statements to Bytecode 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  25. Tell HN: I'm 60 years old. Claude Code has re-ignited a passion 669 upvotes, 543 comments
  26. Palantir and Anthropic AI helped the US hit 1k Iran targets in 24 hours 35 upvotes, 19 comments
  27. PC processors entered the Gigahertz era today in the year 2000 with AMD's Athlon 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. Modernizing swapping: virtual swap spaces 41 upvotes, 33 comments
  29. My application programmer instincts failed when debugging assembler 22 upvotes, 15 comments
  30. Launch HN: Palus Finance (YC W26): Better yields on idle cash for startups, SMBs 54 upvotes, 82 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 7 March 2026. Source here.
